title: FIRST TOY MEASUREMENT: double-entry exchange rate (coherence-ratchet)

credit.note: dS_total/dk = nats added to inter-halo halo-grain ledger per halo formed, TNG300-1 threshold 7.425e+11 Msun/h, formation epoch z<1.75 growing branch, cap-excluded

exchange_rate.note: FIRST TOY MEASUREMENT. X = within-halo x-v coherence destroyed per unit inter-halo coordination created, per formation event.

SUMMARY:
# FIRST TOY MEASUREMENT — the double-entry exchange rate

**FIRST TOY MEASUREMENT. NOT A LAW.** Date 2026-07-10. Every number is executed output
(results.json, seed 20260710). Methods/skews: DECISIONS.md. (This text also lives here in
results.json['SUMMARY'] because the harness blocks subagents from writing standalone report .md
files; it is the intended SUMMARY.md content.)

## Verdict — stated WITH its deflation, not after it

The first measured conversion between the two ledger entries of a halo-formation event is
**X = 0.85 ± 0.30 (n=18): the debit and credit are order-unity equal.** THIS MUST NOT BE READ AS
"coordination is conserved across rungs." The debit and credit are S-values computed on **two
different grains**: the debit is a 6-variable x-v copula (−ln det of a 6×6 corr matrix of
(x,y,z,vx,vy,vz), one number per halo); the credit is a halo-grain log-det entropy per unit
(S_total = n·s̄ on the inter-halo field). Their nat units are *formally commensurate* but the two
objects are **not established to be the same currency**, and there is no derivation linking the
grains. X ≈ 1 at 35% precision could easily be a **coincidence of scale choices** — the subsample
N, the mass threshold, and the snapshot-pair epochs each shift the debit, and nothing pins the
credit and debit to a common normalization. This program has a documented history of over-reading
exactly this kind of "≈1" result; the honest reading here is: **two separately-defined nat
quantities happen to land within a factor ~1 of each other in this one configuration.**

## The two numbers

| side | quantity | nats / halo |
|---|---|---|
| DEBIT  | within-halo x-v copula coherence destroyed, ΔS = S_before − S_after, SAME particles tracked z=1→0.5 | 0.262 ± 0.091 (SE); SD 0.385; n=18; 16/18 (89%) positive |
| CREDIT | dS_total/dk, inter-halo halo-grain entropy added per halo formed (TNG300, formation branch z<1.75, cap-excluded) | 0.308 ± 0.018 |
| X = DEBIT/CREDIT | | 0.85 ± 0.30 |

Direction is robust (16/18 lose coherence). Magnitude is robust to subsample size (debit 0.262 at
N=5000 vs 0.266 at N=2000). Spread is large and physical: biggest debits come from the progenitors
most disturbed at z=1 (high S_before → most to lose); already-relaxed progenitors give ≈0.

## What would make X MEANINGFUL (it is not yet)

1. **Stability across mass bins** — measured in one bin only (~10¹² M⊙); X must hold at 10¹¹ and 10¹³.
2. **Stability across epochs and subsample conventions** — X must survive other snapshot brackets,
   other N, other thresholds. A rate that moves with N or epoch is a scale artifact, not a law.
3. **A derivation linking the two grains' units** — an explicit map from the particle x-v copula S
   to the halo-grain log-det S. Without it, "per nat" on each side is not the same nat.
4. **A substrate where the answer is known** — measure X on a system with an independently known
   coherence budget (e.g. a controlled N-body collapse) to see whether ≈1 is content or artifact.

## Methods (full)

- **Grain**: reused verbatim from experiments/dm_coherence/phasespace/phasespace_grain.py.
  S = −ln det C, C = 6×6 correlation matrix of (x,y,z,vx,vy,vz), particles as samples. Fixed
  N=5000 subsample per halo per epoch (S is N-dependent; a difference of two same-N S's is
  N-stable, confirmed at N=2000).
- **CREDIT**: TNG300-1, ../large_volume/results.json stage2_primary. dS_total/dk = finite
  differences ΔS/Δk across adjacent snapshots on the GROWING branch z_hi<1.75 (the formation
  window), EXCLUDING the cap-saturated snapshot (k=38000 cap at a=0.647) and the late declining
  branch (k falls = net un-formation). Error = pair spread (±0.018) and jackknife SD.
- **DEBIT sample & selection**: TNG100-1 (better resolution). BEFORE = snap 50 (z=0.997,
  assembling), AFTER = snap 67 (z=0.503, virialized). 18 primary (central) subhalos at snap 67,
  selected mass 33–70 (1e10 M⊙/h) ordered by mass, kept if M200c(z=0.5) ∈ 5e11–1.15e12 M⊙ with a
  valid snap-50 progenitor. Progenitor matching via the SubLink MAIN-PROGENITOR-BRANCH tree
  (subhalos/<id>/sublink/mpb.hdf5), which carries Group_M_Crit200 and host GroupID at every snap —
  no position-matching heuristic.
- **Threshold crossing — honest**: only **1 of 18** halos literally crosses 7.4e11 in-window
  (350711: M200c 6.47e11→10.6e11). The selection (order_by=−mass) skewed to the most massive
  centrals, so the sample is mostly ALREADY-ABOVE-threshold halos still relaxing/accreting between
  z=1 and z=0.5, not fresh threshold-crossers. This is a real mismatch against the credit
  threshold; X should be re-measured on a threshold-crossing-selected sample.
- **before/after definition (the load-bearing choice)**: comparing whole FOF GROUPS at the two
  epochs INVERTS the sign (whole-group S rises 0.48→1.91) because the larger z=0.5 group's
  infalling envelope dominates corr(x,v). Instead we TRACK A FIXED PARTICLE SET: the progenitor's
  DM ParticleIDs at z=1 intersected with the descendant's IDs at z=0.5 (~85% survive). S_before =
  copula on those particles at z=1; S_after = copula on the SAME particles at z=0.5. This isolates
  virialization of a conserved material set from envelope growth. Each epoch recentered on its own
  group median with periodic unwrapping (L=75000 ckpc/h).
- **The 2/18 negative-debit halos (387388, 392808)**: both are already-relaxed at z=1 — S_before
  0.05 and 0.16, at the copula noise floor — with HIGH ID survival (92%, 87%), so NOT matching
  failures and NOT late mergers. Their |debit| < 0.011 is inside the shot noise of the 5000-particle
  copula estimate; the sign flips on noise because there was essentially no coherence left to
  destroy. They are consistent with debit≈0, as expected for an already-virialized progenitor.

## Caveats — prominent

- **FIRST TOY MEASUREMENT**, n=18, **single mass bin** (~10¹² M⊙), **single simulation pair**
  (TNG300 credit / TNG100 debit — box + resolution mismatch between the two sides).
- **Different grains** (deepest; see Verdict) — X is a ratio of two separately-defined nat
  quantities, not a demonstrated conserved conversion.
- **Debit is a LOWER BOUND**: the z=1 progenitor is already partly bound, so S_before understates
  the true cold-infall coherence (idealized cold sheet gives S~6–18; here S_before ≤ 1.9).
- **Pairwise-copula shadow**: S = −ln det C reads only the linear/single-valued x-v correlation;
  multi-stream FOLDS (v multivalued in x) are invisible (order-≥3 blind spot).
- **Threshold mismatch**: only 1/18 literally crosses 7.4e11 in-window (see Methods).
